page: Amazon Web Services c7i.12xlarge is a compute-optimized instance built on the Nitro hypervisor. It features 48 vCPUs and 24 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 8488C processor running at 3.2 GHz. The x86_64 architecture includes 105 MB of L3 cache, 2 MB of L2 cache per core, and 48 KB of L1d cache. Memory capacity is 96.0 GB of DDR5 RAM operating at 5 GHz, yielding a 2.0 GB per core ratio. This instance does not include local storage or dedicated GPUs, and it provides a baseline network bandwidth of 19 Gbps.

Benchmark results indicate strong performance across multiple compute-intensive workloads. The instance achieves strong scores in Geekbench single-core and multi-core tests, as well as software compilation and ray tracing. Memory bandwidth is particularly strong for read-only operations, reaching 3865.15 GB/sec for cached small blocks and 203.15 GB/sec for uncached large blocks, though write bandwidth is average at 81.54 GB/sec. Cryptographic and compression tests also show strong results, with a Passmark CPU encryption score of 50522.05 and single-threaded gzip compression at 33.17 GB/sec.

The Intel Xeon processor supports advanced instruction sets, including Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX-512) and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for deep learning and matrix operations. These hardware features enable strong performance in small-to-medium LLM inference, processing 196.44 tokens/sec for prompt processing on a 7B model. The instance is highly suited for compute-bound workloads such as high-performance web serving, database operations, software compilation, and vector calculations. Its qualitative cost efficiency is characterized by high compute density and DDR5 memory speeds, though workloads requiring local NVMe storage or GPU acceleration will require alternative configurations.
description: Amazon Web Services c7i.12xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 48 vCPUs and 24 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 8488C processor running at 3.2 GHz. Built on the Nitro hypervisor, it includes 96.0 GB of high-speed 5 GHz DDR5 memory, providing 2.0 GB of RAM per core. The instance lacks local storage and dedicated GPUs, but delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 19 Gbps. Benchmark data highlights strong performance in software compilation, ray tracing, and database operations, alongside strong memory read bandwidth of up to 3865.15 GB/sec. It supports advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and Intel AMX, making it highly efficient for vector calculations and medium-sized LLM inference. This instance is best suited for compute-intensive workloads, including high-throughput web serving, cryptography, and parallel processing tasks.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c7i.12xlarge is a compute-optimized instance with 48 vCPUs, 96 GB DDR5 RAM, and an Intel Xeon processor, delivering strong performance for compilation and database workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c7i.12xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 48 vCPUs, 96 GB DDR5 RAM, and a 3.2 GHz Intel Xeon processor.
tagline: A high-density compute instance featuring DDR5 memory and advanced matrix extensions for intensive parallel processing and compilation workloads.
bullet_points: 48 vCPUs and 24 physical cores powered by a 3.2 GHz Intel Xeon 8488C processor
96.0 GB of high-speed 5 GHz DDR5 memory (2.0 GB per core)
Advanced instruction support including Intel AMX and AVX-512
Strong performance in software compilation, ray tracing, and database operations
19 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with no local storage or GPUs
